key_insight: This paper introduces a soft robotic exoglove concept that combines mobility assistance with massage-like compression to help reduce hand spasticity and pain.
engineering_relevance: It shows how personalized soft actuators and finite-element-guided design can improve conformability, force distribution, and fit for hand-assistive wearable robotics.
method: Modeled soft pneumatic actuators in FEM under 100 and 200 kPa.
Optimized actuator geometry to reduce stress before fabrication.
Fabricated dorsal, ventral, and palmar actuators and assembled them into a wearable exoglove.
evidence_signal: The prototype was experimentally tested and successfully worn by a pilot user, with reported conformal contact, targeted fit, and initial comfort/effectiveness validation.
watch_item: Results are preliminary and based on a pilot-level evaluation, so clinical performance, durability, and safety under repeated use remain unproven.
paper_title: Towards Soft Robotic Exogloves for Musculoskeletal Manipulation to Reduce Pain and Spasticity
